paper_metadata.title: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models
paper_metadata.abstract: By decomposing the image formation process into a sequential application of denoising autoencoders, diffusion models (DMs) achieve state-of-the-art synthesis results on image data and beyond. Additionally, their formulation allows for a guiding mechanism to control the image generation process without retraining. However, since these models typically operate directly in pixel space, optimization of powerful DMs often consumes hundreds of GPU days and inference is expensive due to sequential evaluations. To enable DM training on limited computational resources while retaining their quality and flexibility, we apply them in the latent space of powerful pretrained autoencoders. In contrast to previous work, training diffusion models on such a representation allows for the first time to reach a near-optimal point between complexity reduction and detail preservation, greatly boosting visual fidelity. By introducing cross-attention layers into the model architecture, we turn diffusion models into powerful and flexible generators for general conditioning inputs such as text or bounding boxes and high-resolution synthesis becomes possible in a convolutional manner. Our latent diffusion models (LDMs) achieve a new state of the art for image inpainting and highly competitive performance on various tasks, including unconditional image generation, semantic scene synthesis, and super-resolution, while significantly reducing computational requirements compared to pixel-based DMs. Code is available at https://github.com/CompVis/latent-diffusion .

extraction_data.organization: Ludwig Maximilian University Of Munich & Heidelberg University, Germany
extraction_data.innovation: Latent diffusion models (LDMs) apply diffusion models in the latent space of pretrained autoencoders, enabling high-resolution image synthesis with reduced computational complexity while preserving quality and flexibility.
extraction_data.application: Image synthesis, inpainting, super-resolution, text-to-image generation
extraction_data.extension: By operating in the latent space of a pretrained autoencoder, LDMs achieve significant computational efficiency while maintaining high visual fidelity and enabling flexible conditioning on text, bounding boxes, and other inputs.
extraction_data.paper_title: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models

extraction_data.organization: Max Planck Institute For Intelligent Systems
extraction_data.innovation: Latent Diffusion Models (LDMs) use a variational autoencoder to compress images into a lower-dimensional latent space, enabling efficient diffusion modeling in the latent space rather than pixel space. This approach improves sample throughput and reduces computational cost while maintaining high-quality image synthesis.
extraction_data.application: Text-to-image synthesis, image generation, semantic synthesis, super-resolution, inpainting
extraction_data.paper_title: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models

extraction_data.organization: Max Planck Institute For Intelligent Systems
extraction_data.innovation: Latent Diffusion Models (LDMs) use a perceptual compression model (encoder-decoder) to map high-resolution images to a low-dimensional latent space, enabling efficient diffusion-based image synthesis. The model conditions on text or other inputs via cross-attention mechanisms.
extraction_data.application: Image generation, conditional image synthesis, text-to-image generation
extraction_data.paper_title: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models

extraction_data.organization: Max Planck Institute For Intelligent Systems
extraction_data.innovation: Latent diffusion models (LDMs) use a diffusion process in a compressed latent space to generate high-resolution images efficiently. The model leverages a VAE encoder to compress images into a latent representation, then applies diffusion in this space, enabling scalable and high-quality image synthesis.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale image datasets (e.g., ImageNet, MS-COCO, CelebA-HQ, FFHQ, LSUN)
extraction_data.application: Image generation, text-to-image synthesis, layout-to-image synthesis, super-resolution
extraction_data.paper_title: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models

extraction_data.organization: Max Planck Institute For Intelligent Systems
extraction_data.innovation: Latent Diffusion Models (LDMs) use a variational autoencoder to compress images into a lower-dimensional latent space, enabling high-resolution image synthesis with diffusion models trained in this compressed space. This approach reduces computational cost while maintaining high-quality output.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale image datasets (e.g., ImageNet, MS-COCO, CelebA-HQ, FFHQ, LSUN)
extraction_data.application: Image generation, text-to-image synthesis, layout-to-image synthesis, super-resolution

The model conditions on text or other inputs via cross-attention mechanisms.
